metadata.description: RAG evaluation test set for SEC filings knowledge base
metadata.note: Ground truths auto-generated by RAG + Claude verification. Run build_test_set.py to populate.

questions.query: What was Microsoft's total revenue in FY2024?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Microsoft's total revenue in FY2024 (fiscal year ended June 30, 2024) was $245,122 million ($245.1 billion), as reported in the FY2025 10-K as a comparative year. This was up from $211,915 million in FY2023 and grew to $281,724 million in FY2025. Note: $247,442 million is the pro forma revenue as if Activision Blizzard had been acquired on July 1, 2022 — not actual revenue.
questions.ground_truth_source: MSFT 10-K FY2025 (filed 2025-07-30) p62/p129 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What is Tesla's total long-term debt as reported in their latest 10-K?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on Tesla's latest 10-K filing for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, Tesla's total long-term debt (net of current portion) is $5,535 million, consisting of $3 million in recourse debt and $5,532 million in non-recourse debt. When including finance leases with a long-term net carrying value of $222 million, the total long-term debt and finance leases equals $5,757 million.
questions.ground_truth_source: TSLA 10-K FY2025 (filed 2026-01-29) p84/p124 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What was NVIDIA's net income in their most recent fiscal year?
questions.ground_truth_answer: NVIDIA's net income for the fiscal year ended January 25, 2026 (FY2026) was approximately $120,067 million, representing 55.6% of total revenue. Total revenue was $215,938 million (Compute & Networking: $193,479M, Graphics: $22,459M), up 65% year-over-year from $130,497M. Operating income was $139,297 million (60.4% of revenue).
questions.ground_truth_source: NVDA 10-K FY2026 (ended Jan 25, 2026) — income statement + segment table, verified from source PDF

questions.query: What was Apple's gross margin percentage in their latest 10-K?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Apple's total gross margin percentage was 46.9% in fiscal year 2025 (ending September 2025), as reported in their 10-K filed October 31, 2025. Products gross margin was 36.8% and Services gross margin was 75.4%, reflecting the higher-margin Services segment pulling overall margins upward.
questions.ground_truth_source: AAPL 10-K FY2025 p36 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What are the main business segments of Amazon?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Amazon reports three business segments in its latest 10-K (fiscal year 2025, filed February 6, 2026): North America (retail, advertising, and subscriptions through North America-focused stores), International (similar services through internationally-focused stores), and Amazon Web Services (AWS, on-demand cloud computing platforms and services). North America contributed $426.3B, International $161.9B, and AWS $128.7B in net sales for FY2025.
questions.ground_truth_source: AMZN 10-K FY2025 p43/p107 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What was JPMorgan's total assets as of their latest annual report?
questions.ground_truth_answer: JPMorgan Chase's total assets as of December 31, 2025 were $2,142,534 million (approximately $2.14 trillion), representing a 21% year-over-year increase from $1.77 trillion in 2024, as reported in their 10-K filed February 13, 2026. Note: this figure reflects total assets per the Selected Metrics summary; the consolidated balance sheet total may differ.
questions.ground_truth_source: JPM 10-K 2026-02-13 p139/p146 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What was Meta's advertising revenue in their most recent 10-K?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Meta's advertising revenue in 2025 was $196.175 billion, representing an increase of $35.54 billion, or 22%, compared to 2024, according to their 10-K filing dated January 29, 2026.
questions.ground_truth_source: META 10-K 2026-01-29 p108/p127 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What was Berkshire Hathaway's operating earnings in their most recent annual report?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on the SEC filing dated 2026-03-02, Berkshire Hathaway reported total operating businesses earnings before income taxes of $51,714 million in 2025. Combined operating earnings before investment gains totaled $42,873 million, which represented a decline from 2024 operating earnings before income taxes of $53,936 million, primarily driven by lower earnings from insurance underwriting operations.
questions.ground_truth_source: BRK-B 10-K 2026-03-02 p182 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: Break down Amazon's revenue by segment for the last reported fiscal year.
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on Amazon's 10-K filing for fiscal year 2025 (ended December 31, 2025), the company's revenue breakdown by segment is: North America generated $426,305 million (59% of total), International generated $161,894 million (23% of total), and AWS generated $128,725 million (18% of total), for consolidated net sales of $716,924 million. North America showed 10% year-over-year growth, International showed 13% growth, and AWS showed 20% growth, with consolidated net sales growing 12% year-over-year.
questions.ground_truth_source: AMZN 10-K FY2025 p43 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: How has Apple's gross margin trended over the past 3 fiscal years?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on the provided SEC filing data, Apple's total gross margin improved consistently over the three-year period from FY 2023 to FY 2025, increasing from 44.1% to 46.9% (280 basis points), with absolute gross profit growing from $169.1 billion to $195.2 billion. Services gross margin showed particularly strong expansion, growing 460 basis points from 70.8% in FY 2023 to 75.4% in FY 2025, while Products gross margin remained relatively flat, ranging from 36.5% to 37.2% across the period.
questions.ground_truth_source: AAPL 10-K FY2025 p36 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What was Alphabet's revenue split between Google Search, YouTube, and Google Cloud in their latest annual report?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on Alphabet's 2025 annual report (10-K filed February 5, 2026), Google Search & other generated $224.532 billion in revenue, YouTube ads generated $40.367 billion, and Google Cloud generated $58.705 billion. Together, these three segments accounted for $323.604 billion of Alphabet's total revenues of $402.836 billion in 2025, representing approximately 80% of total company revenue.
questions.ground_truth_source: GOOG 10-K 2026-02-05 p58/p60 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: How has NVIDIA's data center revenue grown over the past two years?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on NVIDIA's SEC filings, Data Center revenue grew 65% year-over-year for the nine-month period ended October 26, 2025, reaching $131.423 billion compared to $79.606 billion in the prior year period. In the most recent third quarter alone, Data Center revenue was $51.2 billion, representing 66% year-over-year growth. For the full fiscal year 2026, Data Center revenue was up 68% from the prior year.
questions.ground_truth_source: NVDA 10-K FY2026 p67 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What is Goldman Sachs's CET1 capital ratio and how does it compare to their target?
questions.ground_truth_answer: As of December 2025, Goldman Sachs's CET1 capital ratio was 15.6% under the Standardized approach and 20.9% under the Advanced approach. These ratios significantly exceed the regulatory minimum CET1 requirement of 4.5% and the total CET1 requirement (including buffers) of 13.6% as of September 2025. The filings do not disclose an internal target CET1 capital ratio.
questions.ground_truth_source: GS 10-K 2026-02-25 p174/p232 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What were the main drivers of Eli Lilly's revenue growth in their latest annual report?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Eli Lilly's revenue growth was primarily driven by tirzepatide-based products: Mounjaro generated $22,965 million in 2025 (99% increase from $11,540 million in 2024) and Zepbound achieved $13,542 million (175% increase from $4,926 million in 2024). These two products combined accounted for $36,507 million in 2025 revenue, with the company's overall revenue growing 45% from $45,043 million in 2024 to $65,179 million in 2025. In contrast, other products declined 1% and Verzenio grew only 8%, confirming that tirzepatide-based therapies were the dominant growth engines.
questions.ground_truth_source: LLY 10-K 2026-02-12 p76 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What were the reported impairments or write-downs disclosed by Intel in their latest filings?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Intel reported $950 million in asset impairments and accelerated depreciation charges in 2025 related to manufacturing assets with no remaining operational use, plus $460 million in non-cash charges for property and equipment not yet in service. In 2024, Intel recognized $3.3 billion in impairments and accelerated depreciation charges primarily related to Intel 7 process node manufacturing assets, along with $2.3 billion in charges for equipment exceeding projected capacity requirements. As of December 27, 2025, Intel's accumulated goodwill impairment loss totaled $3.9 billion, with $2.6 billion associated with Mobileye, $415 million with CCG, and $303 million with DCAI.
questions.ground_truth_source: INTC 10-K 2026-01-23 p32/p34 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: Compare the R&D spending as a percentage of revenue between Google and Microsoft in their latest filings.
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on the latest SEC filings, Google invests a higher percentage of revenue in R&D compared to Microsoft. Google's R&D expenses were $61,087 million, representing 15% of revenues in 2025, while Microsoft's R&D expenses were $32,488 million out of $281,724 million in total revenue, representing approximately 11.5% of revenues in fiscal year 2025. This represents a difference of approximately 3.5 percentage points, with Google dedicating a larger proportion of its revenue to research and development activities.
questions.ground_truth_source: GOOG 10-K 2026-02-05 p40 / MSFT 10-K 2025-07-30 p64 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: Summarize how semiconductor companies are discussing AI-related demand in their latest earnings reports.
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on the SEC filings, semiconductor companies report strong current AI-driven demand, with AMD experiencing a 36% year-over-year revenue increase in Q3 2025 driven by AI accelerators and processors, while Micron states that AI-driven memory and storage growth is outpacing industry supply. However, companies consistently emphasize significant uncertainty about long-term AI demand trajectories and acknowledge forecasting difficulties, with supply constraints creating allocation challenges across the industry. All major semiconductor firms warn of potential risks including oversupply scenarios and the difficulty in accurately predicting AI-related demand timing and magnitude.
questions.ground_truth_source: AMD 10-K 2026-02-04 p131 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: How are companies in the financial sector discussing AI adoption in their latest filings?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Financial institutions are discussing AI adoption through two distinct lenses in their latest 10-K filings. Most prominently, JPMorgan Chase (p82) and Goldman Sachs (p109, p134) frame AI primarily as a risk management concern: JPMorgan warns of competitive disadvantage if competitors deploy AI more effectively, including potential 'replacement or disintermediation of direct customer relationships if AI agents autonomously manage financial decisions,' while Goldman Sachs dedicates a section to AI risks covering biased or incorrect model outputs, rapidly evolving regulatory uncertainty, AI used by bad actors to commit fraud and cyber attacks, and the complexity of AI models limiting transparency. Goldman Sachs also notes competitors may gain productivity advantages through faster AI integration. By contrast, American Express (p8) takes a more strategic deployment stance, explicitly citing 'uses for generative artificial intelligence (AI) and the integration of our products and services in agentic commerce' as part of its technology platform. Across all institutions, no specific AI investment amounts or quantified financial benefits from AI adoption are disclosed.
questions.ground_truth_source: JPM 10-K 2026-02-13 p82 / GS 10-K 2026-02-25 p109/p134 / AXP 10-K 2026-02-06 p8 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: Among Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Google, Tesla, and Walmart — which shows the strongest financial health based on their latest filings?
questions.ground_truth_answer: Based on the SEC filings presented, **Microsoft and NVIDIA demonstrate the strongest financial health**. Microsoft generated $128.5 billion in operating income for fiscal year 2025 with $94.6 billion in liquidity and only $43.2 billion in debt, while NVIDIA maintains $62.6 billion in cash with just $8.5 billion in long-term debt and $42.8 billion in operating cash flow. Both companies combine substantial liquidity, exceptional cash generation, and modest leverage relative to their peers.
questions.ground_truth_source: NVDA 10-K FY2026 p76/p77 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: Compare cloud segment revenue growth rates: Amazon AWS, Microsoft Intelligent Cloud, and Google Cloud over the last reported year.
questions.ground_truth_answer: For the most recently reported fiscal year, Amazon AWS grew 20% year-over-year to $128.7 billion (FY2025 vs. $107.6B in FY2024), per AMZN's 10-K filed February 6, 2026. Microsoft's Intelligent Cloud segment (which includes Azure, server products, and enterprise services) grew approximately 19-21% to roughly $87-88 billion in FY2025, per MSFT's 10-K filed July 30, 2025. Google Cloud grew 29% to $58.7 billion in 2025 (vs. $45.4B in 2024), per GOOG's 10-K filed February 5, 2026. Among the three, Google Cloud had the fastest percentage growth rate, while AWS generated the largest absolute revenue.
questions.notes: Cross-company cloud segment comparison — note: Azure not broken out separately, use Intelligent Cloud segment
questions.ground_truth_source: AMZN 10-K FY2025 / MSFT 10-K FY2025 / GOOG 10-K 2026-02-05 p49 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: What are the most significant legal or regulatory risks disclosed across the big tech companies in their latest 10-K filings?
questions.ground_truth_answer:
# Major Legal and Regulatory Risks Across Big Tech Companies

The most significant legal and regulatory risks disclosed in big tech companies' latest 10-K filings include antitrust enforcement actions (such as the EC's €3.0 billion fine against Google for self-preferencing and €798 million fine against Meta for abuse of dominance), substantial data privacy obligations under GDPR and similar regulations (including Meta's €1.2 billion fine for unlawful data transfers and Google's $1.4 billion privacy settlement), Digital Markets Act compliance requirements with €200 million in fines imposed on Meta, and emerging AI regulation including the EU's AI Act. Additionally, semiconductor companies face significant export control restrictions limiting data center GPU sales to multiple regions, and Intel received $5.7 billion in accelerated CHIPS Act disbursements while issuing 275 million shares of common stock pursuant to a Department of Commerce agreement.
questions.ground_truth_source: AAPL 10-K 2025-10-31 p49 / MSFT 10-K 2025-07-30 p63 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: Compare operating margins between Visa and Mastercard over the last two fiscal years.
questions.ground_truth_answer: Visa's operating margin declined from 65.7% in fiscal 2024 to approximately 60% in fiscal 2025, a decrease of 5.7 percentage points driven by a 30% increase in operating expenses. Mastercard's operating margin improved from 55.3% in 2024 to 57.6% in 2025, a gain of 2.3 percentage points despite a 10% increase in operating expenses. Visa maintained a higher absolute operating margin at approximately 60% compared to Mastercard's 57.6% in their most recent fiscal periods.
questions.ground_truth_source: V 10-K 2025-11-06 p24 / MA 10-K 2026-02-11 p70 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: How are energy companies XOM and CVX discussing the energy transition and capital allocation in their latest annual reports?
questions.ground_truth_answer: ExxonMobil (XOM) has committed to achieving net-zero Scope 1 and 2 emissions in its integrated Permian Basin operated assets by 2035 and plans a 20-30% reduction in corporate-wide greenhouse gas intensity by 2030 compared to 2016 levels, with a dedicated Low Carbon Solutions business segment investing in carbon capture and storage, hydrogen, ammonia, and lithium. Chevron (CVX) takes a more cautious approach, stating it is "not on track to achieve the aspiration by 2050" for upstream production net-zero emissions and has removed 2050 as its timeline, instead emphasizing flexibility to change ambitions based on market conditions, strategy, and financial factors, while focusing on growing its oil and gas business alongside lower-carbon intensity operations.
questions.ground_truth_source: XOM 10-K 2026-02-18 p71 / CVX 10-K 2026-02-24 p40 — verified from source PDF

questions.query: Give me an overview of recent performance of Palantir, including revenue growth, margins, and key business developments.
questions.ground_truth_answer: Palantir demonstrated strong financial performance in 2025 with revenue of $4,475,446 thousand, representing 56% year-over-year growth from 2024 revenue of $2,865,507 thousand. The company expanded its gross margin to 82% in 2025 from 80% in 2024, with gross profit increasing 60% to $3,686,269 thousand. Palantir's business is structured around three main revenue streams: Palantir Cloud (subscription-based hosted software with operations and maintenance services), On-Premises Software (term licenses bundled with O&M services), and Professional Services (on-demand support including user support, configuration, training, and data modeling).
questions.ground_truth_source: PLTR 10-K 2026-02-17 p95 — verified from source PDF
